architecture_gap: The current system cannot allocate capabilities dynamically, route proposals structurally, or coordinate execution through machine-readable task dependencies.
data_model_changes: Add agent profiles, proposal registry entries, task dependency nodes, and council decision records.
governance_implications: Council decisions gain durable machine-readable records for arbitration and replay.
invention_concept: Convert the AAS from a document-only operating manual into a governed execution system with ACR, proposal registry, and task graph surfaces.
migration_strategy: Use bootstrap registries first; evolve toward a persistent service surface later without invalidating stored state.
problem_statement: Capability-aware assignment and proposal-driven execution were impossible.
proposed_architecture_change: Introduce a bootstrap ACR, proposal registry, council decision records, and task graph snapshots under /AAS/runtime/state.
protocol_implications: Proposal routing becomes deterministic and auditable. Task generation becomes machine-readable rather than purely narrative.
security_implications: Stronger traceability improves auditability and post-failure reconstruction.
title: Stateful Registry and Task Graph Core
